title: Build an app from your phone in AI Studio
summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build a custom image-generation app directly from a mobile browser using Google AI Studio, voice commands, and the Nano Banana API. The tutorial shows real-time code generation without requiring software installation.
writeup: A hands-on demonstration of building a fully functional React image-generation app directly from a mobile phone browser using Google AI Studio, with no installation required. The demo produces a custom stuffed-animal configurator for a 5-year-old by dictating app requirements via voice input, then watching AI Studio scaffold a complete React project with CSS styling and Imagen API integration. The through-line is that browser-based AI coding environments now lower the floor for app creation to anyone with a phone and an API key.
key_points: Google AI Studio (aistudio.google.com) runs entirely in a mobile browser with no app install, accepting both typed and voice-described prompts to scaffold a full React application on demand.
The scaffolded app consisted of at least two files (index.css for styling and a React entry point), with AI Studio making autonomous styling decisions based on the stated audience (a 5-year-old).
The app was configured on first launch to prompt the user for a Google AI Studio API key, keeping credentials out of the generated code and enabling the Imagen API call at runtime.
Imagen API (misheard as 'Nano Banana' in captions) is called to generate a custom image from user-selected options (animal type, color, accessories), returning a rendered image within the app.

The voice prompt workflow transcribes and reformulates the spoken description into an optimized prompt before sending it to AI Studio, reducing the friction of composing precise instructions on a small keyboard.
The chat/thinking panel in AI Studio surfaces the model's reasoning and lists files as they are created, giving the builder visibility into the scaffolding process without requiring any code edits.
